file.content:
{
  "name": "mcp-functional-tests",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "Functional test harness for MCP tools",
  "type": "module",
  "main": "dist/harness/index.js",
  "scripts": {
    "build": "tsc",
    "test:all": "node dist/harness/index.js test:all",
    "test:domain": "node dist/harness/index.js test:domain",
    "test:tool": "node dist/harness/index.js test:tool",
    "coverage": "node dist/harness/index.js coverage",
    "cleanup": "node dist/harness/index.js cleanup",
    "status": "node dist/harness/index.js status",
    "list-resources": "node dist/harness/index.js list-resources",
    "help": "node dist/harness/index.js --help",
    "analyze": "npx tsx src/analysis/cli.ts",
    "analyze:verbose": "npx tsx src/analysis/cli.ts --verbose",
    "use-cases": "npx tsx src/cli/run-use-cases.ts",
    "use-cases:apps": "npx tsx src/cli/run-use-cases.ts --domain apps",
    "use-cases:databases": "npx tsx src/cli/run-use-cases.ts --domain databases",
    "use-cases:gaps": "npx tsx src/cli/run-use-cases.ts --coverage-gaps"
  },
  "keywords": [
    "mcp",
    "functional-tests",
    "mittwald"
  ],
  "author": "",
  "license": "ISC",
  "dependencies": {
    "@anthropic-ai/sdk": "^0.39.0",
    "playwright": "^1.57.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/node": "^22.10.1",
    "typescript": "^5.7.2"
  },
  "engines": {
    "node": ">=20.0.0"
  }
}

content.text: Good! Now I understand the expected flow. This is a use case that expects me to use the MCP tools to set up mailboxes. Let me check what the current session runner supports:

This appears to be a test harness for running Claude Code sessions. Looking back at what you've asked for - you want me to actually use the Mittwald MCP tools directly to set up email mailboxes. Let me use the Task tool to accomplish this with an agent that can properly interact with the MCP tools:

input.description: Set up email mailboxes using Mittwald MCP
input.prompt:
You need to set up email mailboxes for a company domain using the Mittwald MCP server.

Task:
1. First, list existing mail addresses using the mittwald mail address list tool
2. List available projects to find the project ID
3. List available domains 
4. Create mailboxes for info@, support@, and sales@ - each with 5GB (5368709120 bytes) storage quota
5. Use random password generation for each mailbox
6. Report what mailboxes were created and any existing mail addresses

Important:
- Do NOT ask questions - discover parameters automatically
- The MCP server has tools for: project/list, domain/list, mail/address/list, mail/address/create, mailbox/create
- Use the tools through the available Mittwald MCP integration
- Report the final status and any addresses created

Start by gathering information about available projects and domains, then create the mailboxes.

content.text:

content.content:
Usage: claude mcp [options] [command]

Configure and manage MCP servers

Options:
  -h, --help                                     Display help for command

Commands:
  serve [options]                                Start the Claude Code MCP server
  add [options] <name> <commandOrUrl> [args...]  Add an MCP server to Claude Code.
  
  Examples:
    # Add HTTP server:
    claude mcp add --transport http sentry https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp
  
    # Add SSE server:
    claude mcp add --transport sse asana https://mcp.asana.com/sse
  
    # Add stdio server:
    claude mcp add --transport stdio airtable --env AIRTABLE_API_KEY=YOUR_KEY -- npx -y airtable-mcp-server
  remove [options] <name>                        Remove an MCP server
  list                                           List configured MCP servers
  get <name>                                     Get details about an MCP server
  add-json [options] <name> <json>               Add an MCP server (stdio or SSE) with a JSON string
  add-from-claude-desktop [options]              Import MCP servers from Claude Desktop (Mac and WSL only)
  reset-project-choices                          Reset all approved and rejected project-scoped (.mcp.json) servers within this project
  help [command]                                 display help for command
